what_it_does: Registers a DNA.gallery module named drawer-tokens that loads a tokens.json object (TOK), builds a drawer UI of color/number/slider controls, synchronizes a warmth dial across multiple UI locations, live-injects CSS variables into gallery mockup iframes, and provides save/export/reset actions. It also publishes helper functions onto the shared module context and exposes a few global shims for legacy HTML onclick/iframe onload call sites.
observations: TOK is fetched once and mutated in place; Save/Export serialize the whole object so untouched fields are preserved.
applyTokens writes CSS variables onto an iframe document's <html> inline style, not the parent root.
Controls are explicitly marked live (immediately drive iframes) or persist-only (saved but not injected).
Warmth is coordinated across tk-warmth, sw-warmth, and grammar/layout drawer mirrors via setWarmthEverywhere.
Re-apply targets both .frame iframe and .thumb iframe elements.
Row factory functions attach _rehydrate closures to DOM nodes for later refresh.
The module depends on DNA.gallery, DNA.api, DNA.color, DNA.surfaces, DNA.injectVars, and ctx hooks applySpaceFrame/onFrameApplied.
Global shims window.applyTokens and window.toggleDrawer are kept for HTML attributes.
standouts: The live-drive model distinguishes fields that map to CSS vars from fields that are merely stored.
Centralized warmth synchronization across multiple independent slider elements.
Rehydrate hooks stored on generated DOM rows for control refresh after reload.
concerns: Many $(id) calls assume elements exist with no null guards.
setWarmthEverywhere unconditionally references window.renderGrammar and window.lyRender if drawers are open.
applyTokens silently returns if iframe.contentDocument is inaccessible, e.g. cross-origin.
Button event listeners are wired immediately at module init, assuming the DOM is already ready.
Global shims may collide with other modules or future code.
notes: This is the token-engine drawer: it owns the loaded token object, renders its editor, and pushes token values into iframe mockups in real time.
questions: What is the exact schema of tokens.json?
What contract does DNA.injectVars expect for var names and warmth?
What do ctx.applySpaceFrame and ctx.onFrameApplied do?
Are .thumb iframe elements actually present in the gallery?
When and by what mechanism is DNA.gallery.register init called relative to DOM readiness?
How are live vs persist-only fields enumerated and kept in sync with mockup CSS?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it extracts the token engine, owns TOK, live-drives iframes, manages warmth, and publishes shared helpers.
apparent_themes: Live design-token preview inside isolated iframe documents.
Mutation-in-place persistence with full-object round trips.
Separation of live CSS-driven fields from saved metadata fields.
Shared runtime context over global namespace for module integration.
Backward-compatible global shims for inline HTML handlers.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn a previously inline token editor into a reusable gallery module that lets a designer tune tokens and immediately see them reflected across mockup iframes, while keeping save/export semantics intact.
novelty: The explicit live-vs-persist-only control classification and the row-level _rehydrate closures are distinctive; the warmth synchronization across multiple independent UI locations is also notable.
contribution: It contributes token loading, in-place token mutation, live CSS variable injection into iframe mockups, warmth dial synchronization, control generation with rehydration, save/export/reset actions, and shared context exports for downstream gallery modules.
summary_for_embedding: A gallery drawer module that loads tokens.json, renders editable token controls, synchronizes a warmth dial, and live-injects CSS variables into mockup iframes while supporting save/export/reset.
intention_for_embedding: To extract the token-engine UI from a monolithic HTML file into a reusable gallery module that lets designers tune tokens and preview them live across iframe mockups.

symbols.does: Returns a DOM element by id.
symbols.description: A small helper wrapping document.getElementById, used throughout the module to grab drawer controls and UI elements.

symbols.does: Computes surface colors for a given warmth value.
symbols.description: Delegates to DNA.surfaces(TOK, warmth) to derive the page/warm/cool/nested surface colors used by the tone inspector and payload builder.

symbols.does: Reads the current warmth value from the drawer slider or tokens fallback.
symbols.description: Returns the numeric value of the tk-warmth input if present, otherwise falls back to TOK.dials.warmth.default or 50.

symbols.does: Pushes token CSS variables into a single iframe document.
symbols.description: Writes the current TOK values as CSS custom properties onto the iframe's <html> element via DNA.injectVars, then calls ctx.applySpaceFrame and ctx.onFrameApplied if available.

symbols.does: Re-applies tokens to all visible mockup and thumbnail iframes.
symbols.description: Iterates over every .frame iframe and .thumb iframe in the document and calls applyTokens on each.

symbols.does: Updates the tone inspector swatches with computed surface colors.
symbols.description: Computes surfaces from the current warmth and writes the hex values and background colors into the tone inspector UI elements.

symbols.does: Creates a color input row for a token field.
symbols.description: Builds a DOM row containing a color picker, label, role text, and hex text field; supports live or persist-only updates and registers a _rehydrate closure.

symbols.does: Normalizes and stores a hex color value and refreshes UI.
symbols.description: Nested inside tkColorRow; validates the hex via normHex, writes it back into the token object, syncs the picker and text fields, and optionally live-drives the iframes.

symbols.does: Creates a numeric input row for a token field.
symbols.description: Builds a label, optional hint, and number input that mutates the token object on input; used for type scale and spacing values.

symbols.does: Creates a range slider row for a token field.
symbols.description: Builds a labeled slider with a value display that mutates the token object on input; used for radius and texture opacity values.

symbols.does: Clears and rebuilds all drawer control groups from TOK.
symbols.description: Empties the grouped containers and uses the row factories to create color, number, and slider controls for accent, ink, surface, semantic, type, space, radius, and texture fields.

symbols.does: Returns the accent color section of TOK.
symbols.description: A getter closure defined alongside ink, surf, and others in tkBuildControls to provide stable object accessors for the row factories.

symbols.does: Synchronizes warmth value across all warmth controls and live-drives previews.
symbols.description: Clamps the value, updates tk-warmth and sw-warmth, refreshes value labels, updates the tone inspector, applies tokens to shown iframes, and triggers grammar/layout renderers if their drawers are open.

symbols.does: Initializes warmth slider event handlers.
symbols.description: Sets the drawer warmth slider to the token default, seeds setWarmthEverywhere, and wires oninput handlers for both tk-warmth and sw-warmth.

symbols.does: Rebuilds controls, binds warmth, and refreshes the tone inspector.
symbols.description: Convenience function called after tokens load to fully refresh the drawer UI state.

symbols.does: Constructs the full save/export payload from TOK.
symbols.description: Captures the current warmth, computes derived surface colors, writes them back into TOK.color.surface, and returns the whole mutated token object.

symbols.does: Shows a transient message in the drawer toast element.
symbols.description: Displays a message with optional error styling and clears any previous toast timer after a fixed delay.

symbols.does: Saves the current token payload via the API.
symbols.description: Builds the payload, applies tokens to shown iframes, calls DNA.api.saveTokens, and shows a success or error toast.

symbols.does: Downloads the current token payload as tokens.json.
symbols.description: Builds the payload, creates a Blob, triggers a download of tokens.json, and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Reloads saved tokens and refreshes the UI and previews.
symbols.description: Calls loadTokens to re-fetch tokens.json, rehydrates controls, and re-drives the open mockups to saved values.

symbols.does: Fetches tokens.json and initializes the drawer.
symbols.description: Uses DNA.api.tokens to load TOK, calls tkHydrate, and applies tokens to any frames already on screen.

symbols.does: Opens or closes the token drawer.
symbols.description: Toggles the tkdrawer open class and tkbtn on state, and closes the grammar drawer if it is open.
